steps.title: Locate the simple subject
steps.content: The subject noun phrase is “Latviaʼs 4,917-word constitution”—a singular noun. The phrase “in contrast to Chileʼs, which was enacted in 1980 and contains a far greater number of words” provides comparison and context, but as a parenthetical element, it doesn't alter the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular verb form; here, the rule is subject-verb agreement. “Ranks” (choice A) is the only singular form. Choices B, C, and D all employ plural verb constructions, violating agreement with the singular subject. That matches choice A — A is correct.
